jimmy@Mac ~ % claude
▗ ▗   ▖ ▖  Claude Code v2.1.123
           Opus 4.7 (1M context) · Claude Max
  ▘▘ ▝▝    /Users/jimmy

❯ can you tell me a story?

⏺ Sure — here's a short one.

  ---
  The Lighthouse Keeper's Last Night

  Old Tomas had tended the lighthouse on Skerry Point for forty-one years. He knew every groan of the iron stair, every mood of the lamp, every shape the fog took as it rolled in from the north.

  On his last night before retirement, a small boat appeared in the bay — too small for those waters, too late in the season. Tomas climbed to the lamp room and turned the beam toward it. The boat held a single
  passenger: a boy of maybe ten, rowing badly, soaked to the bone.

  Tomas met him at the rocks with a blanket and a thermos. "Where did you come from?"

  The boy looked up at the lighthouse, then back at Tomas, and smiled like he'd found something he'd been searching for.

  "My grandfather built this place," he said. "He told me to come see it before they shut the light off. He said the keeper would know him."

  Tomas hadn't told anyone he was leaving. Hadn't told anyone the light was being decommissioned at dawn. He looked at the boy's face — the same stubborn jaw, the same grey eyes as the man who'd trained him
  forty-one years ago, the man who'd been buried for thirty.

  "Yes," Tomas said quietly. "The keeper knows him."

  They sat together until sunrise, and when the light finally went dark, neither of them was alone.

  ---
  Want another? Different mood — funny, eerie, sci-fi?

✻ Churned for 11s
